VoIP. The use of the Internet Protocol (IP) for transmitting voice communications. VoIP delivers digitized audio in packet form and can be used to transmit over intranets, extranets, and the Internet. It is essentially an inexpensive alternative to traditional telephone communication over the circuit-switched Public Switched Telephone Network (PSTN). VoIP covers computer-to-computer, computer-to-telephone, and telephone-based communications.

Freecall, Freephone, 800, 0800 or 1-800 numbers is a special telephone number which is free for the calling party, and instead the telephone carrier charges the called party for the cost of the call. terms.target_term: Telefonieren, treffen, Nachrichten senden und mehr.
terms.definition: tagline for Avaya Cloud Office by RingCentral. Must follow the translation strictly

Feature name. Call Flip lets you transfer or "push" a live call between your devices, including home phones, desk phones, smartphones, using an assigned single digit without any interruption to the call. You need to assign a Flip Number to each device you will use before you can use this feature. Call Flip enables a user to flip an ongoing call between any devices connected to a DigitalLine, including home phones, desk phones, and smartphones that are not equipped with the RingCentral Office app.
Feature name. Call Switch enables switching ongoing calls between RingCentral endpoints, exclusively.

A call transfer is a telecommunications mechanism that enables a user to relocate an existing telephone call to another phone or attendant console, using a transfer button or a switchhook flash and dialing the required location. The transferred call is either announced or unannounced. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Call_transfer for more details

Feature name. Call forwarding, or call diversion, is a telephony feature of all telephone switching systems which redirects a telephone call to another destination, which may be, for example, a mobile or another mobile or another telephone number where the desired called party is available. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Call_forwarding for more details.
to go to a specific part of the video recording
an email sent out to test that the emails look correctly
a template that the user previously created in another webinar and saved
The results of a poll question. The host can share with attendees.
a poll that allows the user to select more than one answer

terms.notes: The context is like 'Your port in order can't be proceeded since duplicate port in number is detected in your order.' 'port in' replaces the word 'transfer' in the new release of Service Web.

terms.definition: An audio library that contains only audio files for the account
Anyone who has access to the admin interface

Return calls (callbacks) that are reserved for specific agents. Also known as a reserved callback or reserved agent callback. Please note that this differs from ‘callback’ in that a callback can go to any agent whereas an agent callback goes to the specific agent assigned to the callback
A way to identify an agent's status, such as Available or On Break.

A product menu option in the left nav bar of the admin interface that provides configuration settings for designing scripts, creating and uploading knowledge base articles, adding phone book entries, and composing email templates
A short audio file that plays a message to the agent as they connect with a customer. The audio may inform the agent about the incoming call, or prompt the agent to accept the call
Configurable alert settings that notify users of condition-based real-time events
Tools for analyzing contact center goals, statistics, metrics, and data
Automatic Number Identification: The phone number of the incoming caller
An attribute is a text or numerical data element that is used to segment the data metrics that you want to analyze
Located in the Settings section and accessible anywhere you can configure audio settings, this is where global and account audio files are stored, configured, and managed
A record of all events performed on the platform
The ability to engage in voice inbound and outbound calls and/or voice calls and digital interactions
Any portion of a call in which the call is transferred or switched to another party/destination

A selection ('bucket') of phone numbers with different area codes that you can purchase from us and assign to any campaign(s). They can be used to match each lead you dial with a phone number in the same (or similar) area code as the lead you are dialing.

1. The background located in Script Studio upon which you can build the logic and flow of a script
 2. The background located in Historical reporting where you view your reports and KPIs
A product menu option that can be accessed via the icon with three multi-directional arrows. This section allows you to manage your digital channels and community configurations
A product menu option in the admin interface that deals with phone number management — from TFN and DID numbers to viewing and managing blocked ANI numbers
Embeddable pop-up windows that facilitate communication with customers and can be added to any custom webpage. Chat widgets can be assigned to digital queues

Designated outbound agents (typically active on HCI- and ManualDial-enabled accounts) whose function is to click a button that sends calls to other agents in order to assist in compliance with industry regulations
A destination that receives inbound calls from a cloud-based unified queue that distributes calls to multiple destinations and/or contact centers
A feature that allows users to configure cloud routing settings and destinations
A cloud-based unified queue that distributes inbound calls to multiple destinations and/or contact centers
The act of releasing a voice call or digital interaction from a queue and routing it to a destination or agent
A product menu option in the left nav bar of the admin interface that provides settings for configuring external web services
Configurable groups of (outbound) agents that are categorized according to the dialing mode being used (Predictive, Preview, etc.)

A product menu option in the left nav bar of the admin interface that provides settings related to outbound calls. Here, you can create dial groups and campaigns, upload lead lists, set up campaign criteria and campaign schedules to limit dialing, and more. This option can be accessed via the phone icon in the left-hand navigation bar
A line of digital interactions needing response from an agent. Digital queues can determine the flow of messages to agents based on a set of routing priorities and rules.
A label used to document, track, and report on call or case outcomes
Do Not Call — A list provided by the National Do Not Call registry that telemarketers are prohibited from contacting
A tool that ‘scrubs’ (or removes) numbers in the Do Not Call registry from campaign lead lists loaded into the platform
Dialed Number Identification Service — Provides a way to see the phone number a customer dialed to reach the call center. Useful for contact centers with multiple inbound phone numbers

Identifies an external application (e.g., a website) that you can configure to launch when an agent is connected to a call. The external application is identified by an HTTP URL. You can also append data tags to the URL from system-generated call and agent information. This allows the external application to bring agents to a page referenced by the data tags you provide

1. A term used to describe the person to ask for when dialing a lead (e.g., the lead’s receptionist or assistant)
 2. A destination that can be used for mapping additional lead information on a lead list during the list file upload process. This destination can be mapped to the column on the lead list that contains the above information. Alternatively, it can serve as an additional auxiliary field that holds whatever data you wish

An agent who makes and takes calls without logging into the agent interface. Ghost agents may utilize another system’s ACD and receive calls, but no information will be sent to their computer. A group of ghost agents will appear as a single agent in our system, so you’ll only be able to record the number of calls sent to the group and not an individual agent. You will also be unable to see which calls went to which agent

A library of standard audio files that is available throughout the platform and can be accessed by all accounts. Audio files can be native to the system, but files can also be uploaded by users and shared to the global audio library
A phone book that is available to all your accounts. Any phone numbers in the global phone book will be accessible by admins on any of your accounts
A means of organizing accounts, campaigns, agents, administrators, and more. Groups can be created to easily configure multiple similar platform elements at the same time
Human Call Initiator — A dialing mode in which one agent (a ‘clicker agent’) manually clicks a button to dial a phone number
Calls that come into a contact center
The built-in software found in the agent interface that functions as a software-based version of a standard landline or mobile phone
A digital task distributed to an agent in Routing mode
A sliding tray setting located under the IVR product menu option. This is where you access settings to create, configure, and build IVRs
The drag-and-drop interface that allows you to use nodes to build a visual representation of potential actions for callers
A KPI (Key Performance Indicator) returns the value of a performance metric for your contact center
A lead information field indicating the number of times a dial attempt has been made on the lead

A field that indicates the order in which a lead will be dialed. The system automatically assigns all leads a default priority number of 999, but you can assign your own priority to a lead by adding a ‘Lead Priority’ column to your lead list that indicates your preferred priority for each lead
The dialable status of a lead. This status can be affected by a number of factors, including the lead's pass count and DNC status

Also called the left nav bar, the blue sidebar that you can always find on the left-hand side of your screen. Here, you’ll find a series of icons and, when expanded, a list of product names associated with each icon. These icons, called product menu options, act as shortcuts to the main parts of the platform
A Role that gives the owner(s) access to all of a company’s accounts
Manual outbound dialing refers to calls dialed via the dial pad in the agent interface. Agents can use the dial pad to make 'one-off' calls, or calls not related to any campaigns or queues

A way to create links, or system 'connections,' to your lead data. In order for the system to recognize and access your data, you must tell it where to find that data. When you map your data, you create a link between the system's labeling conventions and your lead data
The maximum number of times the system will attempt to connect to a lead
A computational expression of numerical or quantitative data
A tool in IVR Studio that determines what happens along the various paths calls can take
The central office code, which is a three-digit code indicating the three numbers that follow the area code in a ten-digit telephone number. Also known as ‘Exchange’
A status in which an agent's phone line is active and ready to start making and/or taking calls. Also known as an ‘Offhook Session’ in Admin or ‘Connected’ in Agent
The coaching signal you can select to play at the start of every agent offhook session
A period in a campaign's schedule during a day (or night) that indicates a campaign will be open (that is, available for dialing) during that timeframe

A lead that does not belong to any list or campaign. Leads can become orphaned when a list is deleted while it is still in the process of uploading. Orphaned leads will not be dialed until they are moved to a list or a campaign
Outgoing calls that are made by agents, dialers, or other software
The toolbar in IVR Studio that contains the nodes used for building IVRs

An event wherein the system or an agent attempts to connect to a lead. One attempt equals one pass (different from manual pass). Please note that there are times in which a manual outbound dial attempt may not result in a pass. This can occur when the person being dialed is not a lead or is not somehow linked to a lead in the system
The minimum number of minutes the system should wait before re-dialing a call when no live person answers on the previous attempt

Payment Card Industry compliance — A set of data security standards (PCI DSS) created by the Payment Card Industry Security Standards Council (PCI SSC) aimed at ensuring account security during payment transactions. These standards ‘apply to ANY organization, regardless of size or number of transactions, that accepts, transmits or stores any cardholder data.’ Our platform offers a set of tools to help businesses maintain PCI compliance. Contact your CSM for more details on tools we offer that can assist you in staying PCI compliant
The level of access granted to a user, as applied to selected products, features, etc.
A label used to document, track, and report on the nature of an incoming message
A label used to document, track, and report on case outcomes. Also known as a disposition
Groups of queues that you rank in terms of their priority for dequeueing calls and messages
Settings that allow you to choose what you want the system to do when specific conditions exist, such as when the queue is closed, or when it has reached its maximum length
A line of inbound calls. Refers to the way incoming voice calls and digital messages are organized
A series of sequential routing events that an inbound voice call or digital message will experience when placed in queue
The order in which agents are contacted when calls are dequeued
When a voice call or digital message is returned to a queue

terms.target_term: Verknüpfung für erneutes Einreihen in Warteschlange
terms.definition: A way to quickly redirect voice calls or digital messages to a queue
An agent that has been flagged as the designated agent for a specific lead, typically in order to ensure that the system will only dial that particular lead for the indicated agent
Representational State Transfer –– An architectural style that facilitates communication between computer systems via the internet by using a stateless protocol

Ring-No-Answer –– A status indicating that an agent failed to answer an inbound call. The system will not send another call to the RNA agent until they indicate they are ready to start taking calls again
Permissions that grant users the ability to access or configure various parts of the platform
Full, unlimited access to the entire platform
The first user on the system; one who has full access to the entire platform and whose access and permissions cannot be removed, limited, or edited by anyone else on the platform (except a superuser)

1. The process of sending inbound voice calls and digital messages to agents
 2. A product menu option in the left nav bar of the admin interface that provides settings related to inbound voice calls and/or digital interactions.
A dynamic, interactive prompt for agents that can be followed and filled out while on a call
A drag-and-drop interface where admins can create and configure dynamic agent scripts

The components of a call based on its consecutive states such as an IVR session or queue. For example, a caller might first interact with an IVR (segment 1), then an agent (segment 2), and then another agent (segment 3)
Secure File Transfer Protocol –– A secure network transfer protocol that provides file access, transfer, and management over a secure channel
A component of a SIP call that relays information, or metadata, about the call. SIP headers are typically composed of a sequence of fields that contain discrete pieces of information

A combination of custom skills that you can assign to each agent. These skills are then managed at the queue level to tell the system to route certain inbound voice calls or digital messages to agents based on their abilities
Service-Level Agreement — A contract defining the level of service expected by the service provider

A secondary navigation menu that slides out from the left-hand navigation bar whenever you click on (or hover your mouse over) one of the product menu options. The sliding tray contains a list of configuration settings specific to each product menu option
Your Customer Success Manager (or another platform employee) who has the ability to manage all users, including root users

1. A product menu option in the agent interface that allows supervisors to monitor agent activity.
 This set of features may also be called ‘Supervision’
 2. Any person whose role is to supervise the activity of agents and the status of interactions. A supervisor can be an agent with supervisory permissions and/or they can be an admin user. Supervisors are sometimes called ‘managers’
One or more people identified for contact by the system, an admin, or agents
In quota management, the maximum number of leads matching your criteria that you want the system to dial
External software that can be integrated into the agent interface to serve as a software-based version of a standard landline or mobile phone
A string with an assigned and identified meaning that you can use to customize your scripts and surveys with variables, such as a client's name, address, and telephone number

Configurable events (like call recording start/end, Pages, custom JavaScript, and web services) used to give scripts a sequential structure. They contain elements and processes that help guide the agent through a call or chat
A collection of available tools located at far left in Script Studio
A specified destination for routing tracked numbers
A set of destinations that can be used by a track number to route a call to the closest destination based on a caller's area code
A phone number that can be used to forward calls to one or multiple destinations using either a set sequence, parallel ringing, or track locator
Unique Item Identifier — A globally unique identification number
Allows administrators to flag certain ANIs that call in frequently and configure special queue events for these callers
A queue for inbound voice calls

When an agent transfers a call to another agent and remains on the line after the transfer is made. The transferring agent can confer with the second agent privately first and then merge both calls to allow the customer in when they're ready to do so
Web-based applications that can be invoked to distribute and/or act upon indicated data and/or program functions
Allows an app or service to stream real-time data to other applications or services. Other services or applications can use the real-time data at various points in a call's lifecycle

terms.notes: when it doesn't refer to channel name in RingCX products, it's translatable.
